text: You arrive in a remarkably quiet sector. The sensors pick up nothing but distant starlight. It's a rare moment of peace.
This node is completely empty. No planets, no asteroids, no ships. Just the vast emptiness of deep space.
As soon as you exit the jump, alarms blare. A Rebel fighter was waiting in ambush!
A ship with pirate markings suddenly decloaks off your port bow. They are charging weapons!
You spot a cluster of cargo pods floating in an asteroid field. It looks like they were jettisoned in a hurry.
You find a heavily damaged, abandoned mining station. A quick scan reveals some usable military supplies left behind.
A friendly civilian trader hails you: 'You look like you're going a long way. Trade you 4 Fuel for 15 Scrap?'
A black-market arms dealer offers you a pristine Burst Laser II, but the price is a steep 45 Scrap.
You arrive at a distress beacon to find a civilian ship under attack by a pirate interceptor!
You find an intact derelict ship drifting silently. There are no life signs, but the cargo bays might still be full.
Your crew finds a hidden stash of supplies in the captain's quarters!
It was a trap! A localized booby trap explodes, damaging your hull as your crew frantically retreats.
A research station is engulfed in flames. A frantic transmission begs for someone to repair their fire suppression system.

choices.text: [Engi Crew] Send your Engi to remotely slice the system.

text: Your crew braves the flames and manages to save the scientists! They reward you with their research funds.
The fire spreads too fast! Your crew barely makes it back to the airlock, taking minor structural damage in the escape.
Your Engi effortlessly bypasses the station's damaged relays and activates the foam suppressors. The grateful scientists reward you.
A heavily armed slaver ship hails you: 'We have prime workers for sale! Give us 25 scrap, or we'll take your crew by force!'

choices.text: We don't negotiate with slavers. Open fire!

text: A pirate ship is hiding inside an asteroid field, waiting for unsuspecting travelers. They attack!
You find a strange, glowing alien monolith orbiting a gas giant. It emits a low, pulsating frequency.

choices.text: Bring it aboard and study it.

text: The monolith releases a burst of nanites that rapidly repair your ship's hull plating before crumbling to dust.
The monolith is highly unstable! It explodes in the cargo bay, severely damaging the hull.
You study it for hours, but it's completely inert. It's just a glowing rock.
You jump too close to a highly volatile star! To make matters worse, an enemy scout is moving to intercept.
You find a ship drifting. They hail you, coughing heavily. 'A plague has hit our crew. Please, help us!'

choices.text: [Medbay Lvl 2] Bring them aboard and cure them.
Keep your distance. We can't risk infection.

text: Your advanced Medbay makes quick work of the pathogen. Grateful, one of their specialists offers to join your crew.
You arrive in an ion storm. Your reactor power is severely limited. Suddenly, a ship decloaks!
You find an escape pod. Inside is a Mantis warrior, heavily armed but unconscious.
The Mantis wakes up and, recognizing you saved its life, pledges its blades to your cause.
The Mantis wakes up in a frenzy and immediately attacks your crew! You have no choice but to fight back.
Sensors detect a buried weapons cache on a nearby moon. You'll need high-powered lasers to cut through the crust.

choices.text: We don't have the firepower. Leave it.

text: You are hailed by a space station. 'Help! We're being overrun by giant alien spiders!'

choices.text: Send the crew in to help (Risky).
[Boarding Drone] Send in an automated drone.

text: Your crew fights bravely and exterminates the arachnid threat. The station rewards you handsomely.
The spiders are too much! Your crew flees back to the ship, but one of them was left behind in the chaos...
The boarding drone mercilessly clears the station without risking your crew. The survivors hand over their remaining supplies.
A well-armed mercenary ship hails you. 'We can scout the sector for you or hold off the Rebels... for a price.'

choices.text: Pay them to scout the sector.
Pay them to delay the Rebels.

text: A Slug light cruiser attacks you unprovoked! As you charge weapons, they hail you: 'Wait! We surrender! Take these supplies and spare us.'

choices.text: Never trust a Slug. Open fire!

text: Surprisingly, they keep their word and transfer some scrap and fuel before jumping away.
It was a ruse! As you lower your shields to receive the transfer, they power up their weapons and target your engines!
You find a heavily shielded trading post broadcasting its inventory across all frequencies.
Sensors pick up a lone life sign on a barren moon. It appears to be a stranded pilot who has gone completely mad from isolation.

choices.text: [Level 2 Medbay] Bring him straight to the Medbay.

text: After some food and rest, his sanity returns. He offers to join your crew out of gratitude.
He seems calm at first, but suddenly snaps! He attacks a crew member and steals some scrap before escaping in an escape pod.
Your advanced medical facilities sedate and cure his psychological trauma. He eagerly joins your crew as a skilled engineer.
An unmanned Rebel scout drone is patrolling this beacon. It hasn't spotted you yet, but it's positioned close to the jump point.
You manage to slip past the drone's sensor range undetected. You are clear to jump.
The drone's sensors pick up your engine emissions! It powers up its weapons and sends out a distress signal to the Rebel fleet.
On your second jump through this sector, you intercept a distress ping from a disabled science vessel. Their crew has survived but they need immediate assistance.

choices.text: Ignore the ping and move on.
[TELEPORTER] Beam your crew aboard to assist and salvage.
Dock and offer what supplies you can spare.
